summary:
Chlorhexidine mouthwash reduces Streptococcus mutan levels when used intensively over two weeks; this approach targets acute bacterial control necessary before transitioning into more sustained care strategies as seen commonly recommended practices targeting similar oral health issues including cavities prevention among others where such rapid action might be beneficial but not indefinitely maintained due practical considerations related patient compliance etc.. 

Key Points Summarized In Two Sentences:
Chlorhexidine mouthwash significantly decreases Streptococcus mutans counts through intense twice weekly usage lasting approximately one-to-two weeks; it serves an essential role during initiation phases aiming at quick microbiological impact prior shifting onto long term management plans suitable beyond these immediate benefits achieved from concentrated application cycles designed mainly against active infections or high-risk scenarios involving rampant plaque formation leading potential tooth decay risks.* Agent1 summarized that using chlorhexidine mouthwash every day for two weeks can help reduce harmful bacteria called *Streptococcus mutans*, which causes tooth problems, making sure teeth stay healthy after treatment ends. This method works best right away because keeping up strict hygiene afterward helps maintain good results without needing constant strong treatments all time.*
*Note:* Adjusted summary language based provided instructions ensuring clarity & conciseness appropriate per requirement constraints given original response length limitations noted earlier regarding brevity needed here too.
